content.problemStatement:
You are given a sequence of integers a1,a2,...,aNa_1, a_2, ..., a_Na1​,a2​,...,aN​. An element ak is said to be an average element if there are indices i,ji, ji,j (with i≠ji \neq  ji=j) such that ak=ai+aj2a_k = \frac{a_i + a_j}{2}ak​=2ai​+aj​​.

In the sequence

37102217153 \quad 7 \quad 10 \quad 22 \quad 17 \quad 153710221715

for i=1,j=5i=1, j=5i=1,j=5 and k=3k=3k=3, we get ak=ai+aj2a_k = \frac{a_i + a_j}{2}ak​=2ai​+aj​​. Thus a3=10a_3 = 10a3​=10 is an average element in this sequence. You can check that a3a_3a3​ is the only average element in this sequence.

Consider the sequence

37103183 \quad 7 \quad 10 \quad 3 \quad 183710318

With i=1,j=4i=1, j=4i=1,j=4 and k=1k=1k=1 we get ak=ai+aj2a_k = \frac{a_i + a_j}{2}ak​=2ai​+aj​​. Thus a1=3a_1=3a1​=3 is an average element. We could also choose i=1,j=4i=1, j=4i=1,j=4 and k=4k=4k=4 and get ak=ai+aj2a_k = \frac{a_i + a_j}{2}ak​=2ai​+aj​​. You can check that a1a_1a1​ and a4a_4a4​ are the only average elements of this sequence.

On the other hand, the sequence

381117303 \quad 8 \quad 11 \quad 17 \quad 3038111730

has no average elements.

Your task is to count the number of average elements in the given sequence.

Input:The first line contains a single integer NNN indicating the number of elements in the sequence. This is followed by NNN lines containing one integer each (Line i+1i+1i+1 contains aia_iai​). (You may assume that ai+aja_i + a_jai​+aj​ would not exceed `MAXINT` for any iii and jjj).

Output:The output must consist of a single line containing a single integer kkk indicating the number of average elements in the given sequence.
